scope_boundary: Finite synthetic burst-review workload only: selected protected capacity smoothing reduces modeled overload and residualizes deferrals while rejecting review erasure. It does not prove real load stability, TokenMana behavior, production scheduler behavior, or reviewer-capacity optimization.
transition_reason: The load-stability probe is stronger than a pure prose claim because it records a finite workload, selected route, baseline route, negative review-erasure control, residualized deferrals, and a Lean/Python fixture bridge. It remains synthetic and local, so it blocks promotion for real load-stability, deployed scheduling, review-capacity optimization, TokenMana, or economic claims.
required_artifacts: live or externally reviewed load-stability workload
negative_results: no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: synthetic workload described as real load stability
support-state promotion without a separate accepted transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed load-stability claim after live or externally reviewed workload traces, production queue logs, reviewer-capacity measurements, residual/displaced-cost accounting, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent scheduler, queueing, economics, or workload-quality audit
non_claims: does not promote Resource Economics above argument
does not prove real load stability
does not prove deployed scheduler, TokenMana, PlanForge, reviewer-optimization, or economic behavior
does not create an upward support-state transition
